name: Cosmic strings (topological)
summary: One-dimensional topological defects formed during cosmological phase transitions when a U(1) symmetry breaks (or its discrete subgroup of higher-rank symmetries). Strings have tension G mu ~ (M_GUT/M_Pl)^2 ~ 10^-6 for GUT-scale strings. Produce stochastic GW backgrounds, lensing arcs, and CMB temperature discontinuities. Can also seed structure formation, though at currently-allowed tensions they contribute < few % of structure power.

addresses_puzzles.note: Pre-inflation, cosmic strings were proposed as alternative seeds for structure formation; now disfavored by CMB observations which are dominantly inflationary. Strings can still contribute up to G mu < 10^-7 (CMB) - 10^-11 (PTA) without violating bounds.

touches_benchmarks.note: GUT-scale cosmic strings produce a stochastic GW background detectable by NANOGrav, EPTA, IPTA. Recent NANOGrav 15-yr results are consistent with G mu ~ 10^-11 - 10^-12.
G mu < 10^-7 from CMB temperature anisotropies (Planck); strings can be subdominant contribution without changing peak positions.

new_fields: U(1) gauge field that becomes a string after symmetry breaking
introduces.note: Single new free parameter is the string tension G mu, set by the symmetry-breaking scale.

history: Kibble (1976), 'Topology of cosmic domains and strings', J. Phys. A 9, 1387 — established formation criteria for topological defects in early-universe cosmology. Vilenkin (1985) review systematised cosmological consequences. Active research target for pulsar-timing arrays and CMB-S4.

references.citation: Kibble, 'Topology of cosmic domains and strings', J. Phys. A 9, 1387 (1976)
Vilenkin, 'Cosmic strings and domain walls', Phys. Rep. 121, 263 (1985)
